title: Germany Grants EUR 154m to Back Hydrogen Technology Hubs
content:
Germanyâ€™s ministry for digital and transport has provided grants totalling EUR 154 million (USD 166.3m) to support the establishment of decentralised innovation and technology centres for hydrogen. Of the total amount, EUR 84 million was allocated for a hub in the city of Chemnitz, including a EUR-14-million contribution from the state of Saxony.

The remaining funding was dedicated to the northern German cluster consisting of Bremen, Bremerhaven, Hamburg, and Stade.

The federal financing for the centres comes from the German Recovery and Resilience Plan (DARP) and is provided through the European Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF).
author: Analysts Account
